reason: The statement applies as the text highlights the contrast between the righteous behavior of Jehoshaphat and the ungodly actions of Ahab. Jehoshaphat sought the counsel of the Lord through a true prophet, Micaiah, while Ahab surrounded himself with false prophets who spoke lies. This shows that while Ahab and his prophets were viewed as evil, Jehoshaphat stood out as the righteous one in this context.
